description:
India Book (IB) I, 30.
In this incompletely preserved contract written by Hillel b. ʿEli (fl. 1066–1108 CE), Yosef Lebdi provided merchandise worth 460 dinars with which the active partner Khallūf would trade. Lebdi will receive three-quarters of the profits and Khallūf one quarter. The contract is described as muḍārabat al-tujjār, a merchants' commenda, which normally provides for a division of two-thirds and one third of the profit. Here the active partner did not make an investment on his side and so his share in the profits was lower.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-11. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
